beginner.sentence: The sharpner is on the desk.

elementary.sentence: My pencil is blunt, so I need a sharpner.
I always keep a sharpner in my pencil box.
This sharpner makes my pencils very sharp.
I bought a new sharpner from the school shop.
The sharpner fell under the bench during class.
Please do not throw sharpner shavings on the floor.
My friend gave me a bright yellow sharpner.
There are many types of sharpners in the shop.
I sharpened three pencils with this sharpner.
Be careful when you use the sharpner.

intermediate.sentence: The sharpner that my mother bought is better than the old one.
I have been using this sharpner since the beginning of the school year.
If you forget your sharpner, you can borrow mine during the test.
Could you lend me your sharpner for a moment, please?
Although this sharpner looks simple, it works very well.
The teacher asked us to keep a sharpner in our pencil boxes.
I would rather buy a metal sharpner than a plastic one.
My sharpner broke while I was sharpening my colour pencil.
Have you ever used a sharpner with two different-sized holes?
The sharpner needs to be replaced because the blade has become dull.

upper_intermediate.sentence: The sharpner, which I had been using for over a year, finally broke during the exam.
Had I brought a spare sharpner, I would not have had to borrow one.
Despite being very small, a good sharpner can make a real difference to your writing.
Not only does a sharp pencil write neatly, but it also helps you draw better lines.
I wish I had bought a better sharpner instead of the cheap one that broke.
It is surprising how something as small as a sharpner can be so important in class.
The quality of a sharpner depends on the sharpness of its blade.
Whoever lost their sharpner on the floor should come and collect it.
Were it not for this tiny sharpner, I would not have been able to finish my drawing.
The number of students who forget their sharpners has increased this term.

advanced.sentence: It is remarkable that such an inexpensive tool as a sharpner plays such a vital role in every student's daily routine.
One could argue that the design of the modern sharpner, with its compact size and replaceable blade, represents an efficient solution to a simple problem.
The sharpner that had been sitting in my pencil case for months turned out to be the only one sharp enough to use during the final examination.
No sooner had I lent my sharpner to my classmate than the tip of my own pencil broke.
Regardless of whether students prefer mechanical pencils or traditional ones, a reliable sharpner remains an essential part of any pencil box.

beginner.sentence: My ballpen ran out of ink.
Where did you keep my ballpen?
I found a ballpen on the floor.
The ballpen is inside my bag.
I like this ballpen very much.

elementary.sentence: I always write my homework with a blue ballpen.
My teacher said we must use a black ballpen for the test.
The ink in my ballpen finished during the exam.
I bought a pack of five ballpens from the shop.
Please write your name with a ballpen, not a pencil.
My ballpen stopped working in the middle of the page.
Do not press the ballpen too hard on the paper.
She lent me her favourite red ballpen.
There were no ballpens left in the stationery shop.
I prefer ballpens because they write more smoothly.

intermediate.sentence: The ballpen that I bought last week writes much better than the old one.
I have been looking for my ballpen since the morning.
If the ballpen does not work, try shaking it gently.
Could you please return the ballpen you borrowed yesterday?
Although ballpens are cheap, some of them write very badly.
The teacher told us that we should not use red ballpens in the answer sheet.
I would prefer a ballpen with a grip because my fingers get tired.
My ballpen leaked and left a big ink stain on my shirt.
Have you ever tried writing with a ballpen that has three different colours?
The ballpen needs a refill because the ink has completely run out.

upper_intermediate.sentence: The ballpen, which was a gift from my grandmother, has sentimental value that no other pen could replace.
Had I checked my pencil box before leaving home, I would have noticed that my ballpen was missing.
Despite being the most common writing instrument in classrooms, the ballpen is often taken for granted.
Not only did the ballpen leak all over my notebook, but it also stained the cover of the textbook underneath.
I wish I had kept the cap on the ballpen, as the ink has now dried out completely.
It is frustrating when a brand-new ballpen stops working after just a few pages.
The invention of the ballpen revolutionised the way people write, making fountain pens less common.
Whoever took my ballpen from the desk should return it before the next class.
Were it not for ballpens, students would still be dipping nibs into inkwells.
The number of ballpens discarded each year contributes significantly to plastic waste.

advanced.sentence: It is worth noting that the humble ballpen, invented in the 1930s, transformed everyday writing by eliminating the mess and inconvenience of fountain pens.
One might argue that the widespread availability of cheap ballpens has led to a throwaway culture where writing instruments are treated as disposable rather than treasured.
The ballpen that I had been searching for all morning was eventually discovered wedged between the pages of my science textbook.
No sooner had the teacher distributed the question papers than my ballpen ran out of ink, leaving me in a state of panic.
Regardless of advances in digital technology, the ballpen continues to be an indispensable tool in classrooms around the world.

beginner.sentence: I read a book every night.
My favourite book is about animals.
I left my book at home.
Open your book to page ten.
The book is on the shelf.
I got a new book for my birthday.

elementary.sentence: I finished reading the book before the bell rang.
My teacher gave us a new English book this term.
The pages of this book are torn and dirty.
I always cover my books with brown paper.
Please return the library book by Friday.
There are twelve chapters in this book.
She borrowed my science book and forgot to return it.
Do not write anything on the pages of the textbook.
I enjoy reading books about space and planets.
The book fell from the table and the cover came off.

intermediate.sentence: The book that my father bought me last week is about the history of India.
I have been reading this book for two weeks and I am still on the third chapter.
If you read one book every month, you will read twelve books in a year.
Could you recommend a good book for someone who enjoys adventure stories?
Although the book looks boring from the cover, the story inside is actually very exciting.
The teacher suggested that we should read at least one book during the holidays.
I would rather read a story book than a textbook during my free time.
My book got wet because I left it near the open window when it rained.
Have you ever read a book that changed the way you think about something?
The book needs to be returned to the library because it is already overdue.

upper_intermediate.sentence: The book, which was written by a famous Tamil author, has been translated into more than twenty languages.
upper_intermediate.grammar_focus: non-defining relative clause + present perfect passive

upper_intermediate.sentence: Had I started reading the book earlier, I would have finished it before the exam.
Despite being published over a hundred years ago, this book remains popular among young readers.
Not only does reading books improve your vocabulary, but it also strengthens your imagination.
I wish I had taken better care of my books, as many of them are now damaged beyond repair.
It is widely believed that children who read books regularly perform better in school.
The number of books in our school library has doubled since the new principal arrived.
Whoever finds the missing library book should hand it over to the class monitor.
Were it not for books, much of the knowledge from ancient times would have been lost forever.
The book I was given as a prize turned out to be one of the most interesting stories I have ever read.

advanced.sentence: It strikes me as remarkable that a single well-written book can shape the thoughts and values of an entire generation of young readers.
One could argue that the gradual shift from printed books to digital screens has both advantages and disadvantages for the development of reading habits in children.
The book that had been sitting on my desk for weeks, collecting dust, turned out to be exactly the one my teacher recommended for the summer reading list.
No sooner had I reached the final chapter of the book than the power went out, forcing me to wait until morning to read the ending.
Regardless of whether one prefers fiction or non-fiction, the act of reading a book demands a level of concentration and imagination that few other activities can match.

beginner.sentence: The eraser is inside the box.
I bought a new eraser today.

elementary.sentence: I made a mistake, so I used my eraser.
My eraser smells like strawberry.
This eraser does not rub out ink marks.
I always carry a spare eraser in my pencil case.
Please do not break the eraser into small pieces.
She gave me a pink eraser shaped like a heart.
There are many colourful erasers in the shop.
I rubbed too hard and tore the paper with the eraser.
My eraser keeps slipping off the desk during class.
Do you prefer a soft eraser or a hard one?

intermediate.sentence: The eraser that I bought from the book fair works much better than the one I had before.
I have been looking for my eraser since the maths period started.
If you press too hard with the eraser, you will tear the paper.
Could you please return the eraser you took from my desk?
Although erasers are meant to correct mistakes, some of them leave ugly smudges on the page.
The teacher reminded us to bring an eraser for the drawing class.
I would rather have a good eraser than a fancy pencil.
My eraser wore down completely because I had to correct so many mistakes.
Have you ever seen an eraser that can rub out ballpen ink?
The eraser needs to be soft enough to clean the paper without damaging it.

upper_intermediate.sentence: The eraser, which was barely the size of my thumbnail, somehow lasted the entire examination.
Had I not had a spare eraser in my bag, I would have had to submit the paper with all my mistakes.
Despite being one of the cheapest items in a pencil box, a good eraser is surprisingly hard to find.
Not only did the eraser fail to remove the mark, but it also smeared graphite all over the page.
I wish I had not lent my eraser to my classmate, as he returned it in a terrible condition.
It is ironic that the more mistakes you make, the faster your eraser disappears.
The quality of an eraser can be judged by how cleanly it removes pencil marks without leaving residue.
Whoever keeps breaking erasers into tiny pieces and throwing them around will be given detention.
Were it not for the humble eraser, every pencil mistake would be permanent.
The number of erasers that go missing in a classroom is astonishing.

advanced.sentence: It is often overlooked that the eraser, a seemingly insignificant item, gives students the confidence to attempt difficult problems knowing they can correct their mistakes.
One might argue that the eraser embodies an important lesson for young learners: that making mistakes is a natural part of the learning process and can always be corrected.
The eraser that had been wedged between the pages of my notebook for months was discovered just when I needed it most during the final examination.
No sooner had I finished correcting my answer than the eraser slipped from my fingers and rolled under the bench in front of me.
Regardless of how advanced digital tools become, the simple eraser will likely remain a staple in classrooms for generations to come.

beginner.sentence: I forgot my notebook at school.
The notebook is in my bag.

elementary.sentence: I always write the date on top of each page in my notebook.
My teacher checked all our notebooks after the class.
The pages of my notebook are getting torn and messy.
I need to buy a new notebook for science.
Please write neatly in your notebook.
There are four different notebooks in my school bag.
She copied the notes from my notebook.
Do not tear pages out of your notebook.
I keep a separate notebook for English grammar.
My notebook got wet in the rain and the ink spread everywhere.

intermediate.sentence: The notebook that I use for English has a bright orange cover.
I have been writing in this notebook since January.
If you keep your notebook neat, it will be easier to revise before the exam.
Could you show me how you organised your notes in your notebook?
Although the notebook looks thin, it actually has two hundred pages.
The teacher told us to leave two lines between each answer in our notebooks.
I would rather use a ruled notebook than a plain one for writing.
My notebook fell into a puddle and half the pages became unreadable.
Have you ever kept a notebook just for writing your own stories and poems?
The notebook needs a new cover because the old one has completely torn off.

upper_intermediate.sentence: The notebook, which my grandfather gave me on my birthday, has become my most treasured possession.
Had I labelled my notebooks properly, I would not have brought the wrong one to class.
Despite being filled with scratchy handwriting and doodles, my old notebook brings back many fond memories.
Not only did the notebook get soaked in the rain, but some of the most important notes were washed away completely.
I wish I had written more carefully in my notebook, as I cannot read some of my own notes now.
It is interesting to see how a student's handwriting improves by comparing the first and last pages of their notebook.
The condition of a student's notebook often reflects their attitude towards learning.
Whoever left their notebook on the library table should collect it before it is thrown away.
Were it not for my well-organised notebook, I would have struggled to revise for the final exam.
The number of notebooks a student uses in a year depends on how much they write in class.

advanced.sentence: It is fascinating to look back at old notebooks and trace the gradual development of one's handwriting, vocabulary, and ability to express complex ideas.
One could argue that the notebook serves not merely as a record of lessons taught, but as a personal archive of a student's intellectual growth over the course of an academic year.
The notebook that had been gathering dust on my shelf for years turned out to contain the very notes I needed for my project on Indian history.
No sooner had the teacher announced a surprise test than every student frantically began flipping through their notebooks in search of the relevant chapter.
Regardless of whether one prefers digital note-taking or traditional handwriting, the physical notebook remains a powerful tool for memory retention and active learning.

beginner.sentence: The pencil is long and yellow.
The pencil is on the table.
I have three pencils in my box.

elementary.sentence: I always keep extra pencils in my bag.
My pencil keeps breaking whenever I sharpen it.
This pencil is darker than the other one.
I finished the test using only a pencil.
Please use a pencil for the rough work.
There were no pencils left in the box.
She drew a beautiful flower with her pencil.
Do not chew on the end of your pencil.
I prefer pencils for drawing because they are easy to erase.
The pencil rolled off the desk and fell on the floor.

intermediate.sentence: The pencil that I was using during the art class suddenly snapped in half.
I have been using the same pencil for nearly three weeks now.
If you hold the pencil correctly, your handwriting will improve.
Could you please pick up the pencil that fell behind your chair?
Although pencils seem simple, they are one of the most useful inventions.
The teacher suggested that we use pencils instead of pens for the diagram.
I would rather write with a sharp pencil than a blunt one.
My pencil lead kept breaking because I was pressing too hard.
Have you ever wondered how the graphite gets inside a pencil?
The pencil should be sharp enough to draw thin, clean lines.

upper_intermediate.sentence: The pencil, which appeared to be an ordinary writing tool, was actually a mechanical one with a twist mechanism.
Had I brought a softer pencil, the shading in my drawing would have looked much smoother.
Despite being one of the oldest writing instruments, the pencil continues to be widely used in modern classrooms.
Not only is the pencil useful for writing, but it is also essential for sketching, shading, and technical drawing.
I wish I had not pressed so hard with my pencil, as the deep marks are now impossible to erase.
It is estimated that a single pencil can draw a line approximately fifty-six kilometres long.
The graphite in a pencil determines how dark or light the marks will be.
Whoever sharpened all the pencils before the exam deserves a word of thanks.
Were it not for pencils, the art of sketching and drafting would be far more difficult.
The quality of a pencil can be judged by how smoothly it glides across the paper.

advanced.sentence: It is a testament to the elegance of simple design that the pencil, virtually unchanged for centuries, remains one of the most reliable tools for creative expression and academic work.
One could argue that the humble pencil, with its capacity for both precision and correction, mirrors the learning process itself, where trial and error lead to eventual mastery.
The pencil that had been rolling around the bottom of my drawer for months turned out to be exactly the grade I needed for my still-life drawing.
No sooner had the teacher asked us to begin sketching than I realised that every pencil in my case was either broken or blunt.
Regardless of how sophisticated digital drawing tablets become, many artists maintain that nothing quite compares to the tactile experience of a graphite pencil on paper.

beginner.sentence: The pointer is long and thin.
The pointer fell on the floor.
Do not play with the pointer.
The pointer is near the board.
I can see a pointer on the desk.
The teacher picked up the pointer.

elementary.sentence: The teacher used the pointer to show us a word on the board.
Our class teacher always keeps a pointer beside the whiteboard.
The pointer is made of wood and has a rubber tip.
Please bring the pointer from the staff room.
The pointer broke when it fell off the teacher's desk.
Do not use the pointer to hit the desks or the walls.
The science teacher pointed at the diagram with a long metal pointer.
There is a crack in the middle of the pointer.
Some teachers prefer a laser pointer instead of a wooden one.
The pointer helps the teacher show exactly where to look on the chart.

intermediate.sentence: The pointer that the teacher uses has a small flag attached to the end.
I have never seen a pointer as long as the one our geography teacher carries.
If the pointer breaks, the teacher will probably use a ruler instead.
Could you hand the pointer to the teacher, please?
Although pointers are rarely used by students, they are common in most classrooms.
The teacher explained the map by sliding the pointer from one city to another.
I would rather the teacher use a pointer than tap on the board with a marker.
The pointer slipped out of the teacher's hand and landed on the front bench.
Have you ever noticed that the pointer always seems to end up on the wrong desk?
The pointer should be kept in a safe place so that it does not break.

upper_intermediate.sentence: The pointer, which had belonged to our previous teacher, now sits unused in the corner of the classroom.
Had the teacher brought the pointer to the science lab, explaining the human skeleton would have been much easier.
Despite being a simple stick, the pointer commands attention the moment the teacher picks it up.
Not only does the pointer help the teacher direct our attention, but it also serves as a visual cue to stay focused.
I wish the school had replaced the cracked pointer, as it looks quite unprofessional.
It is interesting how a simple pointer can change the way a class pays attention during a lesson.
The effectiveness of a pointer depends entirely on how the teacher uses it.
Whoever finds the missing pointer should leave it on the teacher's table before the next period.
Were it not for the pointer, the teacher would have had difficulty showing the small details on the world map.
A well-used pointer, combined with clear explanation, can make even the most difficult topic easy to understand.

advanced.sentence: It is worth reflecting on how the presence of a simple pointer in a teacher's hand subtly shifts the dynamics of a classroom, directing collective attention with a single gesture.
One might argue that the pointer is not merely a teaching aid, but a symbol of authority and guidance that has persisted in educational settings for centuries.
The pointer that had been leaning against the blackboard all year finally broke on the very last day of school, as if it too had grown tired.
No sooner had the teacher raised the pointer towards the map than every student in the room instinctively looked in the direction it was pointing.
Regardless of whether classrooms adopt digital projectors or interactive whiteboards, the physical pointer retains a certain directness and simplicity that technology cannot entirely replicate.

beginner.sentence: I draw lines with a scale.
The scale is thirty centimetres long.
My scale is made of plastic.
Where did I put my scale?
Give me your scale for a minute.
The scale is inside my geometry box.
I need a scale to draw this line.

elementary.sentence: I used my scale to draw a straight line in my notebook.
The markings on my old scale have faded.
This scale has both centimetre and inch markings.
I always use a scale when I draw diagrams in science.
Please draw the margin with a scale and a sharp pencil.
There are many colourful scales in the stationery shop.
My scale cracked in the middle when I sat on it by mistake.
Do not use the scale to hit anyone.
A metal scale lasts longer than a plastic one.
The teacher measured the length of the paper with a scale.

intermediate.sentence: The scale that I keep in my geometry box is exactly fifteen centimetres long.
I have been using this scale since the third grade.
If you do not use a scale, your lines will not be straight.
Could you measure this distance with your scale and tell me how long it is?
Although the scale looks ordinary, it is essential for drawing neat diagrams.
The teacher instructed us to underline the heading using a scale.
I would rather use a transparent scale so that I can see through it.
My scale snapped in two while I was trying to bend it.
Have you ever used a triangular scale that architects use for their drawings?
The scale should be placed flat on the paper before drawing any line.

upper_intermediate.sentence: The scale, which I received as part of a geometry set, turned out to be the most frequently used tool in my pencil case.
Had I brought my scale to the exam, I would not have drawn such crooked lines in the diagram.
Despite being a very basic tool, the scale teaches students the importance of precision in measurement.
Not only is the scale used for drawing lines, but it is also helpful for measuring lengths accurately.
I wish I had bought a sturdier scale, as the plastic one broke within the first week.
It is surprising how often students forget to bring their scale to class, even for geometry lessons.
The accuracy of a diagram depends largely on how carefully you place the scale on the paper.
Whoever borrowed my scale from the desk should return it before the drawing period.
Were it not for the scale, drawing precise geometric figures by hand would be nearly impossible.
The steel scale that my father used during his school days is still in perfect condition after thirty years.

advanced.sentence: It is remarkable that such a simple instrument as a ruler can instil in young learners an appreciation for accuracy, measurement, and the mathematical principles that underpin the physical world.

One could argue that the scale represents one of the earliest introductions a child has to the concept of standardised measurement, bridging the gap between intuitive estimation and precise quantification.
The scale that had been sitting at the bottom of my geometry box for the entire semester was the only tool I could find when the teacher announced a surprise diagram test.
No sooner had the teacher asked us to draw the construction than I discovered that my scale had a chip at the zero-centimetre mark, making accurate measurement impossible.
Regardless of whether students eventually move on to computer-aided design tools, the foundational skill of using a scale to draw and measure by hand remains an essential part of early education.
